metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the specific next extraction seam planned for reconcile.py (e.g. 'extract the `_apply_remediation` helper into reconcile_apply.py') so the executor can write the row without guessing.
findings.finding: The plan defers the core design choice of what the 'next planned split seam' for reconcile.py should be entirely to the executor, without naming it or providing a decision rule. This is a 'choose an appropriate X' placeholder — an agent cannot write the architecture.md row without knowing what seam the team intends next.
findings.location: What / files — architecture.md bullet: 'name the next planned split seam'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a concrete line to the What/files bullet, e.g.: 'The next planned seam is extracting `<HelperName>` (~N LOC) into `<target_module>.py`; update the remedy cell to reflect this.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify whether `tests/integration/test_concurrency_regression.py` actually exercises the detached-HEAD-local-ahead git state (HEAD advances, refs/heads/tickets does not); if it does not, either add such a test before updating the citation, or explicitly note in concurrency.md that this specific scenario lacks a Python regression test.
findings.finding: The plan asserts `tests/integration/test_concurrency_regression.py` is a Python successor covering the detached-HEAD-local-ahead scenario from the deleted `test-ticket-sync-detached-head-local-ahead.sh`, but probing the actual Python file finds no test that simulates or names the detached-HEAD git state. The test covers two-clone union, fork tie-break, and concurrent tag adds — none of which reproduce the specific git edge case (HEAD advancing without refs/heads/tickets advancing) that the shell test targeted.
findings.location: Plan 'What / files' section — docs/concurrency.md bullet; docs/concurrency.md:242-243
findings.suggested_fix: Search test_concurrency_regression.py and the broader test suite for any test that does `git checkout --detach` or advances HEAD without the branch ref. If none exists, the concurrency.md WS3 update should say 'no Python successor for the detached-HEAD edge case exists yet' rather than citing test_concurrency_regression.py as the replacement.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify in the plan which concrete seam/function cluster in reconcile.py is the next split target (e.g., a named phase helper or cluster of functions), so the architecture.md remedy row is reviewable and grounded in the current file's structure.
findings.finding: The plan's 'What' directive for architecture.md says to 'name the next planned split seam' for the reconcile.py row, but provides no hint of what that seam is. The implementer must independently discover it from the codebase, and the plan cannot be reviewed for correctness of that seam without it being stated.
findings.location: plan 'What' section — 'refresh the reconcile.py remedy row to current reality and name the next planned split seam'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Identify the specific next split seam for `reconcile.py` in the plan itself (e.g., which phase-helper cluster — preflight, apply-dispatch, binding-state — is the next extraction target) so AC3 is verifiable and the updated row is grounded in the actual planned decomposition.
findings.finding: The plan instructs the implementer to 'name the next planned split seam' for reconcile.py in architecture.md but does not itself identify what that next seam is. With run_differs.py already extracted and reconcile.py still at ~1083 LOC on the allowlist, there is a non-trivial design question about which cluster to extract next — yet the plan leaves this entirely to the implementer. This makes AC3 unverifiable without additional research, and risks the row being written inconsistently with the actual codebase topology.
findings.location: What / files — 'architecture.md: refresh the reconcile.py remedy row to current reality and name the next planned split seam'; AC3
findings.suggested_fix: Add a sentence to the 'What / files' section naming the candidate next seam, e.g.: 'The next seam for reconcile.py is the preflight/status-validation cluster (or whichever cluster the team has identified); the updated row should name that cluster and confirm it clears the 100-LOC floor.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC that requires each removed claim to be accompanied by an inline note (e.g., 'No current test covers this invariant') so the honest-deletion requirement is grep-verifiable.
findings.finding: The plan body describes an honest-deletion path — 'delete the claim where no successor exists (and say so honestly)' — but no Acceptance Criterion verifies that deleted claims are accompanied by an explanatory note. AC1 only checks that remaining citations resolve to existing files; it is satisfied by silent deletion, which contradicts the plan's stated intent.
findings.location: What/files section ('delete the claim where no successor exists (and say so honestly)') vs. AC1 ('Every test file cited in docs/concurrency.md exists in the tree')
findings.suggested_fix: Add: '- [ ] Every claim removed from concurrency.md without a successor test is replaced with an explicit inline note stating no current test covers that invariant (grep-verifiable for a marker such as "no current test" or "TODO").'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Identify and record the specific next split seam for reconcile.py (e.g., which responsibility/class to extract next) in the plan so the AC item 'names the next remedy' is verifiable before the doc change is written.
findings.finding: The plan directs architecture.md to 'name the next planned split seam' for reconcile.py but does not itself identify what that seam is, leaving the AC item unverifiable and risking introducing a new vague or incorrect claim into a load-bearing doc.
findings.location: ## What / files — architecture.md bullet; AC line 3
findings.suggested_fix: Add a line to the plan such as: 'The next seam is [specific extractor/class name], identified by [reasoning or reference to the over-cap analysis].' This makes AC item 3 independently checkable by a reviewer who hasn't read reconcile.py.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify whether any Python test covers the detached-HEAD-local-ahead guard (HEAD ≠ refs/heads/tickets after interrupted rebase); if one exists, cite it explicitly; if none exists, the plan's 'say so honestly' path applies and the concurrency.md WS3 paragraph should acknowledge the gap rather than cite a non-covering test.
findings.finding: The plan names tests/integration/test_concurrency_regression.py as the Python successor for the deleted tests/scripts/test-ticket-sync-detached-head-local-ahead.sh, but that file covers the WS3 failed-push/no-data-loss scenario — not specifically the detached-HEAD-local-ahead scenario (a commit advancing HEAD but not refs/heads/tickets). No Python test covering the detached-HEAD-local-ahead guard was found in the tree.
findings.location: docs/concurrency.md — WS3 section / plan 'What' bullet for concurrency.md

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add tests/interfaces/store/test_push_policy_e2e.py to the plan's enumerated successor files, and update the push-policy citation in docs/concurrency.md to reference it rather than the deleted shell script.
findings.finding: The plan enumerates only two Python successor files (tests/integration/test_store_concurrency.py and tests/integration/test_concurrency_regression.py) for the three dead shell citations, but the push-policy citation's actual Python successor — tests/interfaces/store/test_push_policy_e2e.py — is outside the integration/ directory and is not named anywhere in the plan.
findings.location: docs/concurrency.md — push-policy section ('Pinned by tests/scripts/test-rebar-push-policy.sh') / plan 'What' bullet for concurrency.md
findings.suggested_fix: Replace `Pinned by \`tests/scripts/test-rebar-push-policy.sh\`` with `Pinned by \`tests/interfaces/store/test_push_policy_e2e.py\`` in docs/concurrency.md's push-policy section.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the plan's attribution: for the `docs/concurrency.md` push-policy section (currently citing the dead `test-rebar-push-policy.sh`), the correct Python successor is `tests/interfaces/store/test_push_policy_e2e.py`, not `tests/integration/test_concurrency_regression.py`.
findings.finding: The plan characterises `tests/integration/test_concurrency_regression.py` as covering 'failed-push' (as a successor to `test-rebar-push-policy.sh`), but reading the file reveals the push-policy e2e successor is actually `tests/interfaces/store/test_push_policy_e2e.py`, which explicitly declares itself 'In-process port of the real-origin half of tests/scripts/test-rebar-push-policy.sh'. The plan's attribution of 'failed-push' coverage to test_concurrency_regression.py may lead the author to cite the wrong test file when updating concurrency.md's push-policy section.
findings.suggested_fix: In the plan's 'Why' bullet, change 'failed-push in tests/integration/test_concurrency_regression.py' to 'push-policy modes (always/async/off) in tests/interfaces/store/test_push_policy_e2e.py; no-data-loss on failed push in tests/integration/test_concurrency_regression.py (point d)' to accurately map each deleted script to its actual Python successor.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Provide a concrete proving command for AC1, e.g.: `grep -oE 'tests/[^ )]+\.py' docs/concurrency.md | sort -u | xargs -I{} test -f {} && echo OK`
findings.finding: AC1 says 'grep-verifiable' but supplies no actual command, leaving the executor to invent one. This risks a substring false-positive (e.g. grepping for a partial path that matches a comment rather than an actual citation) and makes the criterion unverifiably correct without a concrete anchor.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC1: 'Every test file cited in docs/concurrency.md exists in the tree (grep-verifiable)'
findings.suggested_fix: grep -oE 'tests/[^ `)]+\.py' docs/concurrency.md | sort -u | while read f; do [ -f "$f" ] || { echo "MISSING: $f"; exit 1; }; done && echo 'All citations exist'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
